name: M.S. in Mechanical Engineering

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: Only a single course is surfaced, ME 791 Mechanical Engineering Colloquium (0 credits), while the two other references (ME 700, ME 701 project/thesis codes) are missing; the taught ME core (thermofluids, solid mechanics, dynamics and controls, design) is entirely invisible so no progression can be traced.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the graduate ME core and specialization electives plus the project/thesis vehicle so a coherent thermal/mechanical/dynamics-and-controls sequence is visible.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 and missingCourseCount is 2 (ME 700 and ME 701 are dangling); the one visible course, the colloquium, carries only a clear 'graduate standing and major in mechanical engineering' restriction, so there is no substantive prerequisite graph.
dimensions.suggestion: Resolve the ME 700/ME 701 project and thesis references and record prerequisites on the taught courses so a real structure appears.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes are present; a lone colloquium listing gives a prospective research student nothing on which to judge the ME competencies the degree builds.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 program learning outcomes describing the analytical, computational, and experimental capabilities of an ME M.S. graduate.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null and no plan is parsed (creditReconcileDelta null), so neither the total (an ME master's is typically ~30 credits) nor any core/elective/thesis split is transparent.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total credit requirement and how it divides among required core, electives, and project/thesis credits.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page shows one zero-credit colloquium, a null total, no plan, and two broken course references, so it cannot orient a prospective student to the degree's content. The single description present is readable but non-representative.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the catalog entry with the full ME course list, a plan of study, and a stated credit total.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: With only the colloquium visible, currency cannot be assessed; mechanical engineering is highly relevant, but the page demonstrates none of the modern thermal, mechatronics, or computational-mechanics content the discipline expects.
dimensions.suggestion: Add and display contemporary electives (mechatronics/controls, computational fluid/solid mechanics, energy systems) so the degree's currency is demonstrable.

descriptionQuality.justification: Only one description is present (ME 791 colloquium), which is clear about the seminar's format and requirement but is a single non-representative sample that cannot establish description quality.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: The one visible course states a sensible 'graduate standing and major' restriction, but two of the three references (ME 700, ME 701) are dangling, so prerequisite clarity across the degree cannot be confirmed.

missingTopics.rationale: A thermal-sciences core is expected of an ME master's but is absent from the surfaced page.

missingTopics.topic: Solid mechanics and finite element analysis
missingTopics.rationale: Continuum/computational solid mechanics is foundational graduate ME content not shown.
Modern ME programs require controls and mechatronics; none is visible here.
Simulation-driven engineering is central to contemporary ME yet unrepresented.
The ME 700/ME 701 project and thesis codes are dangling, leaving the culminating research experience undocumented.

emergingTrends.rationale: Simulation-first mechanical design and digital replicas are reshaping ME research and practice.
Integration of sensing, actuation, and control is a leading ME direction warranting a dedicated course.
Design-for-additive and architected materials are transforming mechanical design.
Electrification, batteries, and thermal management are high-growth ME areas the degree should address.
Data-driven surrogate models and physics-informed ML are entering computational mechanics research.

topSuggestions: Rebuild the catalog page with the department's real graduate ME core and electives, since a single colloquium cannot represent the degree.
State the total credit requirement and the core/elective/thesis split (currently null).
Resolve the dangling ME 700/ME 701 project and thesis references.
Publish 3-5 program learning outcomes for ME analytical, computational, and experimental competencies.
Add modern electives (mechatronics/controls, computational mechanics, energy systems) and display them to demonstrate currency.
